sources_exist.note: Five distinct cited pages resolved, but the canonical CT Acquisitions veterinary-practice-multiples URL failed to return accessible content on repeated opens, affecting both buy_mult and exit_mult. All 7 URL occurrences were audited.

figures_match_sources.note: Digitail exactly reports 39.2% professional use and 69.5% daily/weekly use among triers, but not 25% firm-level material adoption. HappyDoc gives a qualitative PIMS timeline without adoption percentages. CoVet gives 39% of veterinarians aged 55+, not practice-owner age or sale intent. NAPHIA reports $4.74B US premiums and nearly 4% North American dog/cat penetration, not 3.9% of 163.6M all pets. The exact multiple ranges could not be audited at their inaccessible URL.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: Within-role fractions are bounded and appropriately conservative for licensed and physical work. However, current_adoption_pct=0.25 is an undocumented haircut from a February 2024 person-level survey and is stale beyond the frozen 12-month threshold for a 2026 current-adoption input. t50_years=7 is asserted from a nonexistent 5%-to-50% PIMS benchmark rather than derived from observed adoption, despite claiming AI will diffuse faster.

rubric_consistent.note: The active-consolidator metric requires PE-backed platforms, yet the count includes Mars, which the cited page expressly labels not PE; the page says at least 6 institutional platforms, not 7 PE-backed active acquirers, and says distressed Thrive's near-term acquisition capacity is constrained. The owner-age succession bonus is based on unsupported owner demographics and sale-intent claims. These score-affecting failures are decisive for a record only 0.0546 below the pass cut; within-tolerance arithmetic deltas do not cure them.

cross_checks_honest.note: The no-proxy and physical-care terminal logic are candid, but uplift calls adoption genuinely slow based on an unsupported seven-year t50. Pricing converts low pet-insurance penetration into 96% of visits paid out of pocket and assumes insurer reimbursement and no clawback without cited support for those mechanisms; terminal value also cites an uncited 3% visit decline.

source_audits.note: The page names the seven organizations in the record but says 'at least 6 institutional platforms' are actively buying. It explicitly labels Mars as NOT PE and says distressed Thrive has materially constrained near-term capacity. Thus it does not support 7 active PE-backed add-on platforms under the frozen definition.

Repeated direct opens returned no accessible page content. Search indexing indicates a veterinary multiples report exists, but inaccessible/search-only content cannot verify the claimed 2024-Q1 2026 cohort, 6.5-9.5x range, approximate 7.5x median, adjusted-EBITDA normalization, or post-peak compression.

The February 2024 vendor/AAHA survey exactly reports 39.2% of 3,968 veterinary-professional respondents using AI in veterinary settings and 69.5% daily/weekly use among those who tried it. It does not measure the share of US clinics with material firm-level deployment or support the run's haircut to 25%, and it is more than two years old for a July 2026 current-adoption input.

Repeated direct opens returned no accessible page content, so the cited $10M+ adjusted-EBITDA 10.5-14.0x range, 11.5-12.5x median, and post-2022 peak comparison could not be verified at the canonical URL.

The vendor article describes desktop PIMS emerging in the late 1980s-1990s and cloud migration through the 2010s. It gives no 5% early-2000s adoption figure, no 50% adoption in 2018, and no measured 15-year diffusion-to-majority benchmark.

The AI-scribe vendor page says 39% of veterinarians are 55 or older. It does not report the age distribution of practice owners, baby boomers as one-third of owners, 40% of owners considering sale, or the run's 38% of owners aged 60+. It also does not establish the asserted succession shortage.

NAPHIA reports 6.41M US insured pets, $4.74B US written premium, and nearly 4% penetration for North American dogs and cats combined, with separate US rates of 5.46% for dogs and 2.04% for cats. It does not report 3.9% of 163.6M all US pets, 96.1% of veterinary visits paid out of pocket, reimbursement to owners rather than clinics, no cost auditing, or 10-15% wellness-plan revenue.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://digitail.com/blog/39-2-of-veterinary-professionals-use-ai-tools-in-their-practice-digitail-and-aaha-survey/), replace the stale early-2024 person-level survey or supply a documented conversion to current 2026 clinic-level material deployment; then derive inputs.t50_years quantitatively from a comparable adoption series.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://happydoc.ai/blog/the-evolution-of-veterinary-practice-management-systems-from-paper-records-to-ai-powered-intelligence), provide measured PIMS adoption percentages and dates; the qualitative technology timeline cannot support a 15-year time-to-50% analog or seven years remaining.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://co.vet/post/veterinarian-facts/), provide practice-owner age and succession evidence rather than substituting the age of all veterinarians; remove the succession bonus unless a documented shortage is established.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/private-equity-veterinary-2026/), apply the PE-backed and active-add-on definition consistently: exclude non-PE Mars, resolve Thrive's constrained status, and document a qualifying last-24-month add-on for every counted platform.

At inputs.buy_mult.url and inputs.exit_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/veterinary-practice-ma-multiples-2026/), provide an accessible source exposing the cited size bands, transaction vintage, medians, and adjusted-EBITDA-after-owner-comp basis; the canonical page could not be opened.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://naphia.org/news/naphia-news/soi-report-2025/), distinguish insured-pet penetration from share of visits and add evidence for owner reimbursement, absence of payer cost auditing, and wellness-plan revenue before relying on those mechanisms for inputs.pi_dist and inputs.pi_moat.

At cross_checks.terminal_value and inputs.pi_moat, provide sources for the state VCPR distribution, telemedicine boundary changes, and claimed visit decline; the physical/licensure thesis is plausible, but the score and PRESSURED classification currently rely on unaudited assertions.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S, V
